rationale: CLAUDE.md explicitly mandates 60dp minimum / 80dp near-edge touch targets for glove operation. The existing POC's 10dp padding and wrap_content button creates a 48dp-height Button that violates this requirement. Industrial terminals are operated one-handed while the other hand holds product — mis-taps waste time and cause frustration. This is the single most impactful UX requirement for the target hardware.
problemSolved: POC activity_main.xml uses LinearLayout with 10dp padding, a Button with wrap_content (default ~48dp height), and no minimum size constraints on any touch target. Text labels use default font sizes. The layout has no consideration for one-handed glove operation on a 5-inch industrial terminal.
